title: The End of Your First Year
prose: Exams come and go in a blur of ink-stained fingers and half-remembered wand movements, and then, somehow, it's already the last night — the house banners hanging in the Great Hall, the year's points totted up, the food that little bit richer than usual.

On the train home, the countryside blurs past the window, and {{name}} realizes, somewhere around Scotland's edge, that a year ago none of this — the castle, the {{house}} common room, any of it — existed. Now it's hard to remember what came before.

choices.label: Wave the summer off from the platform, already counting down to September.
